name: Infor EAM Enterprise Asset Management REST API
what_it_does: Infor EAM enterprise asset management REST API for asset-intensive industries (utilities, oil and gas, manufacturing, facilities, and transportation) to manage asset lifecycle, work orders, preventive and predictive maintenance, spare parts, calibration, and regulatory compliance — enabling automated asset maintenance orchestration, reliability analytics, condition monitoring integration, and regulatory reporting through Infor's enterprise asset management platform built on Infor's ION middleware platform. Enables AI agents to manage work orders for maintenance task creation and completion automation, handle asset registry for equipment hierarchy and specification management automation, access preventive maintenance for schedule and checklist automation, retrieve predictive maintenance for condition-based alert integration automation, manage spare parts for storeroom inventory and requisition automation, handle calibration management for instrument calibration tracking automation, access safety management for LOTO and safe work permit automation, retrieve reliability analytics for MTBF, MTTR, and failure analysis automation, manage regulatory compliance for FDA, ISO, and utility compliance reporting automation, and integrate Infor EAM with IoT platforms, ERP, and GIS for enterprise asset intelligence automation.
use_cases: Automating work order management for utility and energy asset maintenance automation agents
Managing equipment lifecycle and predictive maintenance for manufacturing reliability automation agents
Tracking instrument calibration compliance for pharmaceutical and regulated industry automation agents
Optimizing spare parts inventory and storeroom management for asset-intensive industry automation agents
not_for: Simple facilities work orders (Infor EAM is enterprise-grade, complex for small facilities teams)
IT asset management (Infor EAM is physical asset management, not IT hardware/software asset tracking)
Fleet management as primary use case (Infor EAM has fleet capabilities but not its core focus)
best_when: A utility, oil and gas company, pharmaceutical manufacturer, or large facility wanting AI agents to automate asset maintenance, reliability analytics, calibration compliance, and regulatory reporting through Infor's enterprise-grade EAM platform.
avoid_when: INFOR ION INTEGRATION REQUIRED: Infor EAM uses ION middleware for integration; automated standard-REST assumption creates integration_middleware_required for data exchange with other Infor products; automated must configure ION BODs for Infor-to-Infor integration. IMPLEMENTATION IS COMPLEX: Infor EAM implementation requires configuration for industry-specific asset hierarchies; automated quick-deploy assumption creates misconfigured_system without proper implementation; automated must engage Infor services for proper setup. REGULATORY MODULES ARE SEPARATE: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 55000, and utility compliance are configurable modules; automated built-in-compliance assumption creates compliance_gap for regulated industries without proper module configuration; automated must configure compliance modules. MULTI-SITE LICENSING: Multi-site deployments require appropriate licensing; automated single-license assumption creates license_violation for multi-facility deployments; automated must size Infor EAM licensing for site count.

auth.notes: Infor EAM uses OAuth2 + SAML for enterprise asset management REST API. REST API with JSON/XML. New York, NY HQ. Infor is Koch Industries subsidiary (acquired 2012). Products: Infor EAM (enterprise), Infor CloudSuite EAM. Industries: utilities, oil & gas, pharma, manufacturing, government, transportation. Competes with IBM Maximo and SAP PM for enterprise asset management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Demo for qualified enterprises; production requires Infor EAM license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user; on-premises perpetual license also available; CloudSuite SaaS option; implementation services extra
pricing.notes: New York NY. Koch Industries subsidiary. Annual subscription or perpetual. Enterprise asset-intensive industries.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; EAM APIs throttled per deployment configuration

known_agent_gotchas: ASSET HIERARCHY IS CRITICAL: Infor EAM uses multi-level asset hierarchy (system > equipment > component); automated flat-asset assumption creates incorrect_parent_child for assets not placed in proper hierarchy; automated must maintain proper asset hierarchy

ION BOD MESSAGES FOR INTEGRATION: Infor EAM integrates with other Infor products via ION Business Object Documents (BODs); automated direct-API assumption creates integration_gap for Infor-to-Infor data exchange needing BOD format; automated must use ION middleware for cross-Infor integration

WORK ORDER STATUS FLOWS: EAM work orders follow defined status workflow (Created → Approved → Scheduled → Active → Complete); automated direct-completion assumption creates workflow_violation for work orders skipping required status steps; automated must follow EAM work order status flow

CALIBRATION REQUIRES INSTRUMENT RECORDS: Calibration management requires instruments configured with calibration specifications; automated assumed-calibration assumption creates missing_calibration_record for instruments without specifications; automated must configure instrument calibration specs before tracking

MULTI-ORGANIZATION SECURITY: Infor EAM uses organization-based security that restricts data visibility; automated single-org assumption creates access_denied for data in different organization codes; automated must authenticate with correct organization context
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise asset management. SOC2, GDPR, FDA-21CFR11. OAuth2. US/EU. Asset, maintenance, and compliance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: EAM platform. SOC2, FDA-21CFR11. OAuth2. US/EU. Asset lifecycle and maintenance compliance data.
